old_claim.note: Precision Machine Tools provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: TWI attributes the joining method to heat from an electric arc and does not identify precision machine tools as required for the 1881 demonstration.

source_shape.source_locator: The timeline says Auguste de Meritens joined lead storage-battery plates with arc heat at Cabot Laboratory in 1881 and that the resulting patents marked the beginning of carbon arc welding.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: TWI records de Meritens joining lead storage-battery plates with arc heat at Cabot Laboratory in 1881.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Electric current supplies the documented heat source; precision_machine_tools either names the wrong material or a later manufacturing aid rather than the joining principle.

ontology_before: electric_arc_welding inherited directly from precision_machine_tools as an origin or component dependency.
ontology_after: electric_arc_welding no longer inherits from precision_machine_tools; the source-pinned scope is represented by its remaining prerequisites.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: electric_arc_welding no longer has a direct prerequisite edge from precision_machine_tools.
would_reject_if: Direct historical evidence showed that precision_machine_tools was an operational prerequisite for the source-pinned electric_arc_welding milestone rather than later context or a parallel practice.
short_reason: Precision machine tools are not part of the documented first arc-welding method.
